stories.title: Mapped: Spain braces for 47C heatwave as tourist hotspots warned over record-breaking weather
stories.summary: Spain braces for a record-breaking heatwave this week, with temperatures set to hit 47C in the southeastern interior, nearing the country’s all-time high of 47.6C in 2021. The extreme heat, driven by a Saharan "heat dome," will push wildfire risks to "extreme" levels across much of the country. Popular tourist spots like Málaga and Catalonia face highs of 45C and 42C respectively, while parts of Spain could surpass North Africa in heat. Authorities warn the dangerous conditions will persist through the week.

stories.title: French modeling scout with links to Epstein found dead
stories.summary: A French modeling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead at his home near Paris on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, was under investigation in France for alleged rape and human trafficking tied to Epstein. Prosecutors opened a probe into his death and plan an autopsy. Siad had denied the accusations and sought to present his side before passing.

stories.title: Will Lionel Messi win the Ballon d'Or, and when will Argentina's World Cup star rejoin Inter Miami?
stories.summary: Lionel Messi’s World Cup final loss on July 19, 2026, ended his 2026 Ballon d’Or eligibility, with the award’s evaluation period closing July 31. FIFA’s 21-day rest rule keeps him off Inter Miami’s pitch until at least August 9. The U.S. broadcast of the final drew 63 million viewers, the most for a soccer match in American history. Argentina’s economic openness is also fueling parallel imports, challenging IP rights enforcement.

stories.title: Garnacho to Aston Villa: What does Emery see in latest ex-Man Utd flop?
stories.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on loan from Chelsea with a conditional obligation to make the move permanent. The 22-year-old Argentine winger completed his medical and is finalizing the deal in Birmingham. Villa manager Unai Emery personally requested the signing, with a four-year contract lined up if conditions are met. The move follows Chelsea's record £117 million sale of Morgan Rogers to Villa earlier this summer.

stories.title: Jasper Philipsen wins stage 17 of Tour de France, Tadej Pogačar protects lead
stories.summary: Belgian Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France on July 22, 2026, from Chambery to Voiron, his first stage victory of the race. Tadej Pogačar retained the overall lead with a 4-minute, 32-second advantage over Remco Evenepoel. Philipsen finished ahead of Mauro Schmid and Olav Kooij in the sprint. Stage 18 on July 23 features a 185.2-kilometer mountain route from Voiron to Orcieres-Merlette.

stories.title: Auto e Moto d’Epoca 2026 expands endurance and motorcycle programme in Bologna
stories.summary: Auto e Moto d’Epoca 2026 returns to BolognaFiere from October 22-25, expanding its historic vehicle programme with over 5000 cars and bikes across 14 halls. A new Icons of Speed exhibition will feature endurance racing legends like the 1989 Sauber-Mercedes C9 and 2001 Bentley EXP Speed 8. The event expects visitors from 40 countries, highlighting the global collector market.

stories.title: Tottenham vs MK Dons LIVE! Pre-season fixture, score, live updates and team news
stories.summary: Tottenham beat MK Dons 1-0 in a closed-doors pre-season friendly at Hotspur Way on Wednesday 22 July 2026. Mateus Fernandes scored the only goal with a first-half volley from outside the box. Both clubs used the match to test new signings and squad depth ahead of the new season. Spurs’ Sandro Tonali and Fernandes started together in midfield.

stories.title: Four teens to appear in court after ‘serious’ Virginia incident
stories.summary: Four juveniles face charges after a violent incident in Virginia, Co Cavan, on July 21, with three people sustaining significant but non-life-threatening injuries. The teens are due in Cavan District Children's Court tomorrow at 10:30am. Gardaí say the probe continues, including searches near Lough Ramor. Authorities have not yet linked this case to separate homicide charges against the same suspects.

stories.title: ResilExec Coaching Launches Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™ -- Completing a Full Career Architecture Platform for Mid-Career Leaders in Healthcare and Enterprise IT
stories.summary: ResilExec Coaching launched Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™, completing a career advancement platform for mid-career leaders in healthcare and enterprise IT. The programs target Associate Directors through VPs navigating external transitions or post-promotion challenges. Venerable Holdings agreed to transfer $4.5 billion in assets to Venerable’s advisory platform under a deal with Guardian Life Insurance. The consolidation aims to improve fund efficiency and policyholder benefits. Hillsboro, Oregon, unanimously urged the Port of Portland to switch to unleaded fuel at Hillsboro Airport by 2030 to cut toxic emissions. Research warns that rapid renewable energy shifts in developing countries can raise household costs and deepen poverty without proper infrastructure and support.

stories.title: Revealed: What Otamendi told Rodri in argument after World Cup final
stories.summary: Nicolás Otamendi and Rodri argued heatedly after the 2026 World Cup final, with Otamendi criticizing Rodri for complaining to officials. Rodri, named Golden Ball winner, responded by telling Otamendi to show respect before they were separated. Real Madrid have reignited interest in Rodri after his tournament-winning performances. Manchester City continue contract talks with the Spain midfielder as his future remains uncertain.

stories.title: Testspiele am Mittwoch: MSV, Hilden und die halbe Oberliga spielen
stories.summary: MSV Duisburg plays against Beerschot VA from Belgium at home in Duisburg on Wednesday, July 22, at 18:00. The match is a test game for the Drittligist ahead of the new Drittliga season. The game will be broadcast live on the YouTube channel of the 3. Liga.

stories.title: "Un cattivo con cui la Juve ha sbagliato...": così l'Italia ha fatto di Romero un campione
stories.summary: Cristian Romero, the Argentine defender who nearly joined Juventus in 2018 for €20 million, is now a top target for Inter Milan to strengthen their backline. The 25-year-old, who played six matches at the 2022 World Cup, was sold by Atalanta to Tottenham for €54 million in 2021 after initially joining for around €20 million. Inter aims to secure Romero as part of their defensive upgrades.

stories.title: Amistoso en Paterna, cuando Gourlay se topó con la realidad
stories.summary: Valencia CF faces Eldense in its second preseason friendly at Paterna’s Antonio Puchades stadium at 8:00 p.m. on Wednesday, July 22. The match, broadcast live on À Punt, follows a closed-door opener against Petro Luanda. Corberán’s side, including new signings, aims to test cohesion before the third game against Castellón on Saturday.

stories.title: Play More of the Games You Love, Wherever You Play with XBOX Backward Compatibility on PC
stories.summary: Microsoft is expanding its Xbox backward compatibility efforts by bringing four classic Xbox games to PC, including Blinx: The Time Sweeper, Conker: Live and Reloaded, Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge, and Fuzion Frenzy. The games are available for purchase and download on PC, and also included with all Xbox Game Pass plans. Players can now play these games on PC and supported handheld devices, with added features such as customizable graphics settings and achievements coming in the coming months. This marks the beginning of a broader effort to preserve Xbox games from the past and bring them to PC over time.

stories.title: Gearóid Hegarty enjoying special days alongside his best friends
stories.summary: Limerick’s Gearóid Hegarty and teammates celebrated their sixth All-Ireland senior hurling title on July 22, 2026, marking a historic sixth medal for the core group. Hegarty’s assist to David Reidy in the final highlighted the tight-knit bond within the squad, with Reidy and Seán Finn set to be groomsmen at Hegarty’s wedding in Portugal soon. The team’s remarkable durability has strengthened their friendship, with many players part of the journey since 2017. They reunite mid-winter after a 14-game season.

stories.title: Beliebte ARD-Quizsendung: Leunaer bei „Gefragt - Gejagt“ - Nach Schicksalsschlag in Jubiläumsshow
stories.summary: A 61-year-old man from Leuna has made it to the final of the popular ARD quiz show "Gefragt - Gejagt" after being given a second chance at life. The show celebrated its 1000th episode this week, marking 14 years of its unique concept. The contestant, who has been giving lectures at the Planetarium Merseburg, will now compete in the final. The outcome of the competition will be revealed in the next episode.

stories.title: Confinan Villa del Prado por un incendio forestal originado en Almorox (Toledo)
stories.summary: A wildfire broke out Wednesday afternoon in Almorox, Toledo, forcing residents of Villa del Prado in Madrid to shelter in place. Authorities evacuated two nearby housing developments and cut the N-403 between Cadalso de los Vidrios and Almorox. Fire crews from Madrid and Castilla-La Mancha are battling the blaze with 19 ground and air units. Residents are advised to avoid the M-501 and M-507 roads as operations continue.

stories.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
stories.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall of their booked holiday home in Co Wexford last weekend. Therese, who reserved the entire property via Booking.com for an overnight stay with her partner and two children, said the owner assured her he wouldn’t be present upon their return. Booking.com confirmed the property has been suspended pending an ongoing investigation. Authorities have not yet commented on the incident.

stories.title: USA: New Jersey registrierte versehentlich mehr als 6500 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler
stories.summary: New Jersey officials reported a software error that mistakenly registered over 6,600 non-U.S. citizens as voters ahead of the 2024 presidential election. Governor Mikie Sherrill called the mistake unacceptable and said fewer than 400 of those registered actually voted. The error occurred between June 2023 and June 2024 during driver’s license and ID applications. State officials are reviewing safeguards ahead of upcoming elections.

stories.title: Manuel Barragán vuelve a actuar después de casi perder la vida: "No hay nada que hacer"
stories.summary: Manuel Barragán, conocido por su personaje cómico, superó un grave accidente tras 17 días intubado en UCI por un paro cardíaco y choque contra una farola. El cómico pasó 40 días hospitalizado tras sufrir un aneurisma en la aorta que agravó su infarto. Su familia vivió angustia extrema durante su recuperación, que él califica de milagro. Barragán ya reanudó su actividad y agradeció al equipo médico que lo atendió.

stories.title: Caída de Instagram: la app de Meta registra problemas de funcionamiento en España
stories.summary: Instagram experienced widespread disruptions in Spain on July 22, with users reporting login failures, missing posts, and message sending issues starting around 4:00 PM local time. Downdetector logged over 200 complaints, primarily affecting the mobile app. Service reportedly stabilized by 5:45 PM, though Meta has not commented on the cause. Users are advised to check Downdetector for real-time updates if issues persist.

stories.title: Frankreich: Waldbrand rast auf Cotignac und andere Orte zu
stories.summary: A wildfire in southern France’s Var department forced nearly 400 people to evacuate as flames raced toward Cotignac and two other towns, scorching 1,700 hectares in hours. Authorities deployed nearly 1,000 firefighters and eight aircraft to battle the blaze, which moved unpredictably down Gros Bessillon mountain. Separately, two firefighters died in a Bordeaux-area blaze that trapped them in their vehicle amid extreme fire conditions. Investigations are underway into both fires’ origins.

stories.title: En direct Football. Le quatrième but du FC Metz face au FC Sochaux-Montbéliard : suivez le sixième match de préparation des Grenats en direct
stories.summary: FC Metz beat FC Sochaux-Montbéliard 4-1 in a pre-season friendly on July 22, 2026, at Biesheim Municipal Stadium. Pape Moussa Fall scored a hat-trick and Jessy Deminguet added the fourth goal for Metz. Sochaux’s Mathis Clairicia netted the consolation strike in the 78th minute. Metz’s strong performance follows two opening wins in their summer preparation.

stories.title: Tottenham vs. MK Dons: how to watch preseason friendly
stories.summary: Tottenham Hotspur hosted MK Dons in a closed-door preseason friendly today at Hotspur Way training center. Portuguese midfielder Mateus Fernandes scored the opening goal with a stunning volley from outside the box. The match, part of preseason preparations, was only available to watch via SpursPLAY. Both Fernandes and Sandro Tonali started for Spurs under manager Roberto De Zerbi.

stories.title: Condannata Ilaria Salis: collaboratori licenziati senza giusta causa, risarcimento da 300mila euro
stories.summary: Italian Eurodeputy Ilaria Salis has been condemned to pay 300,000 euros in damages to two former collaborators she fired without cause. The Milan court's March ruling, in absentia, accepted the ex-employees' claims, citing unjustified terminations after only a few months of work. Salis, elected in June 2024, will present an appeal.

stories.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
stories.summary: Chelsea is close to breaching Uefa's financial rules, having spent between £164m and £210m on player transfers since Xabi Alonso became manager. The club has agreed to a deal with Aston Villa for the loan of Alejandro Garnacho, with a conditional obligation to buy the 22-year-old for a reported £42.7m. This deal comes as Chelsea's financial position has improved, with the club generating a significant amount of revenue from player sales. The club's transfer activity will be closely monitored by Uefa as they continue to balance their books.

stories.title: Hospital apologises after man's death in Galway
stories.summary: Henrique Coelho, 26, died on 20 January 2024, two days after being discharged from Galway University Hospital’s emergency department despite complaints of shortness of breath and chest pain. The High Court awarded his family €35,000 in damages for negligence after a postmortem revealed critical omissions in his care. The hospital issued a formal apology to the family in May 2026. Authorities have not indicated further disciplinary or systemic reviews.

stories.title: Xandro Meurisse percuté par un automobiliste après le contre-la-montre du Tour de France : « La voiture l'a projeté »
stories.summary: Xandro Meurisse, 34 ans, a été percuté par une voiture mardi après le contre-la-montre entre Évian-les-Bains et Thonon-les-Bains. Le Belge de l’équipe Pinarello-Q36.5, qui n’a pas subi de fracture mais souffre de brûlures, a pris le départ de la 17e étape ce mercredi. Son manager a confirmé l’incident, décrivant un choc violent projetant le coureur. Aucune enquête n’a encore été ouverte sur les circonstances de l’accident.

stories.title: Bivacchi in montagna usati come alberghi: il Cai lancia l’allarme sugli abusi sulle Dolomiti
stories.summary: Il Club Alpino Italiano lancia l’allarme per l’abuso crescente dei bivacchi sulle Dolomiti, usati sempre più come alloggi improvvisati invece che come rifugi di emergenza. Episodi di vandalismo, rifiuti e occupazioni improprie hanno costretto a interventi di manutenzione straordinari, con costi in aumento. Il 24 luglio a Pieve di Cadore si terrà un convegno nazionale per discutere soluzioni, tra cui la revisione delle regole d’uso. Le strutture restano aperte senza prenotazione, accessibili solo in caso di necessità reale.

stories.title: Le PSG creuse l’option Charles De Ketelaere
stories.summary: Paris Saint-Germain is accelerating transfer plans with the 2026 World Cup over, targeting midfielder Charles De Ketelaere. The club aims to strengthen its squad before key fixtures on August 12 against Aston Villa in the UEFA Super Cup and August 16 against Lens in the Trophée des Champions. De Ketelaere remains a priority as PSG seeks reinforcements to integrate into Luis Enrique’s system. Monaco’s asking price has so far blocked potential moves.

stories.title: Mamdani U-turn on threat to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani reversed his pledge to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, admitting he lacks authority to enforce the International Criminal Court’s 2024 arrest warrant. Mamdani called on US federal authorities to act, as the US is not an ICC member, while Netanyahu plans a September UN visit. The mayor reiterated his stance that Netanyahu is unwelcome in the city.

stories.title: Mannoia a De Gregori: "Su Gaza non avere idee chiare non te lo perdono"
stories.summary: Fiorella Mannoia ha criticato Francesco De Gregori per le sue dichiarazioni su Gaza, definendole imperdonabili di fronte al conflitto in corso. La cantante ha espresso il suo disappunto durante il festival Gaberiana a Firenze, sottolineando la necessità di chiarezza su temi di portata storica. Mannoia, amica e collega di De Gregori, ha ricordato il suo impegno politico nelle canzoni. Il dibattito riflette le tensioni su come gli artisti dovrebbero affrontare questioni sociali urgenti.

stories.title: 'Would Beckham do it?' - Fury stands by Thailand fight
stories.summary: Tyson Fury will face Mariusz Wach in a non-televised fight in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, with a win setting up a potential showdown with Anthony Joshua. Fury, 37, has won 35 pro fights, with two defeats and one draw. The fight will not be broadcast live, but a win will put Fury one step closer to facing Joshua in November. Fury's comments suggest he is focused on his upcoming bout, rather than seeking a more lucrative event elsewhere.

stories.title: Un secolo di passione azzurra: la sfilata dei campioni e lo show del Plebiscito per celebrare i 100 anni del Napoli
stories.summary: Napoli’s SSC club marks its 100th anniversary with a free citywide festival on August 1. The celebration starts at 7:26 p.m. from Piazza Carità, the site of the club’s first headquarters, with church bells and blue smoke. A one-kilometer parade will wind through central streets, led by club president Aurelio De Laurentiis and local leaders. The event blends sport, music, and tradition to honor the club’s deep ties to the city and its global fanbase.

stories.title: Wilfred Genee-beelden nep? ‘Nee, dan zou hij gelijk ontkennen!’
stories.summary: Wilfred Genee faces fresh scrutiny after viral kroegbeelden showed him with a younger woman in Soest. Johan Derksen defended him in Groeten uit Grolloo, calling the encounter a harmless hug with his ex-children’s nanny. Genee has not responded to the footage aired by RoddelPraat. Mainstream pundits now weigh in as the debate intensifies.

stories.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
stories.summary: Matthis Abline, a 23-year-old attacker, is close to joining AS Monaco in a record transfer deal worth around 30 million euros, surpassing the previous record held by Nathan Zézé. Abline has already agreed to sign with Monaco, and the two clubs are nearing an agreement after months of negotiations. This deal would be a significant transaction in the history of FC Nantes, where Abline currently plays. The transfer is expected to be finalized soon.

stories.title: Preview: Levski Sofia vs Universitatea Craiova - prediction, team news, lineups
stories.summary: Levski Sofia hosts Universitatea Craiova at Georgi Asparuhov Stadium for the first leg of their Champions League second qualifying round tie on Wednesday. The Bulgarian champions, who reached this stage with a 5-1 aggregate victory over Borac Banja Luka, will look to make home advantage count. Levski Sofia dominated their previous qualifier, winning 4-0 in the second leg. A win for Levski Sofia would set them up well for the return leg in Romania.

stories.title: Will Britain’s summer heatwave outdo 1976? Here’s what the data says
stories.summary: Britain’s current heatwave has seen 31 days over 25C so far this summer, nearing the 33 recorded in the record-breaking 1976 heatwave. The dry spell has left eastern and north-eastern England with just half their usual rainfall, while hosepipe bans now cover over two million people. Forecasters warn high pressure will keep conditions hot and largely rain-free for at least another week, with temperatures potentially hitting 37C by July 31. The Met Office says very warm weather could persist into early August, raising the risk of further heat warnings.

stories.title: Gemeinsam gesellschaftliche Resilienz stärken
stories.summary: Up to eight German cities and municipalities will join a new resilience project starting October 2026. The initiative aims to strengthen crisis preparedness by involving local organizations and citizens in a six-month participatory process. Separately, a June workshop in Neustadt Holstein trained 14 people with Multiple Sclerosis in resilience techniques to better manage daily challenges. Both efforts focus on building inner strength to handle stress and adversity.

stories.title: INFO OUEST-FRANCE. À Rennes, une femme de 80 ans violée dans son hall d’immeuble par un jeune de 16 ans
stories.summary: A 16-year-old was arrested in Rennes on July 21, 2026, for the July 4 rape of an 80-year-old woman in her apartment building’s lobby. Police found cocaine in his possession during the arrest. The suspect, a foreign minor, remains in custody with a court hearing set for July 23. Authorities identified him through DNA evidence.

stories.title: L'estate bollente di Bastoni: il mercato, le parole dell'agente, l'Inter che se lo tiene stretto
stories.summary: Alessandro Bastoni remains Inter Milan’s key defender as Saudi club Al-Hilal explores a potential move. No formal offer has been made, and Bastoni is currently considered untouchable by the Nerazzurri. The Saudi transfer rules require Al-Hilal to sell a foreign player before signing another, delaying any immediate deal. Inter’s focus remains on reinforcing their backline, though a record bid could force a rethink.

stories.title: Los cazas F-16 ucranianos pasan a la ofensiva: EEUU confirma el derribo de un Su-35 ruso
stories.summary: Un F-16 ucraniano derribó un caza ruso Su-35S en combate aéreo el 8 de julio, confirmaron fuentes estadounidenses. El general Dan Caine, jefe del Estado Mayor Conjunto de EEUU, validó el hito durante una audiencia en el Senado el 21 de julio. Este primer derribo aire-aire marca un giro en la guerra, donde Ucrania emplea cazas occidentales para misiones ofensivas. La industria ucraniana avanza en capacidades de defensa aérea y combate aéreo.

stories.title: Kyle Kelly and Omari Patrick: Forest Green Rovers sign duo
stories.summary: Forest Green Rovers have signed midfielder Kyle Kelly on a six-month loan from Liverpool and forward Omari Patrick on a permanent deal. Kelly, 20, has eight caps for St Kitts and Nevis, while Patrick, 29, scored seven goals for Tranmere last season. Both moves were confirmed ahead of the new campaign. Kelly will join the squad immediately as they prepare for their opening fixtures.

stories.title: AZ oefent al 23 jaar in Dirkshorn: "Gras moet precies 2,1 centimeter lang zijn"
stories.summary: AZ has trained in Dirkshorn for 23 years, preparing for tonight’s friendly against KRC Genk on a pitch with grass precisely 2.1 centimeters tall. The match starts at 18:30 as AZ aims to sharpen form ahead of the Johan Cruijff Schaal on August 2. After mixed results against Anderlecht and Zulte Waregem, the Alkmaarders seek consistency before facing PSV in the season opener.

stories.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
stories.summary: Spain’s Socialist Party (PSOE) reaffirmed its support for former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on Wednesday amid accusations tied to the Plus Ultra airline rescue, urging the judiciary to determine the facts. PSOE spokesperson Montse Mínguez stated the party has no reason to doubt Zapatero’s account and emphasized the ongoing judicial investigation will clarify the allegations. Agriculture Minister Luis Planas linked recent claims by businessman Julio Martínez to a defense strategy in the case, reiterating Zapatero’s presumption of innocence. The Plus Ultra rescue unfolded during the pandemic under EU-backed liquidity measures.

stories.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
stories.summary: Two trams collided on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge around 11:10 AM Wednesday, leaving 15 injured, including one in critical condition. Emergency crews freed a trapped passenger and treated others on-site or at area hospitals. The crash damaged both trams and closed the bridge to all traffic. Authorities confirmed the toll was revised downward from earlier reports.

stories.title: Watch: Mateus Fernandes scores stunning goal on Tottenham Hotspur debut vs MK Dons
stories.summary: Tottenham Hotspur midfielder Mateus Fernandes scored a stunning goal on his debut during a pre-season friendly against MK Dons on July 27. The Portuguese player struck from outside the box with a volley to open the scoring. Both Fernandes and Sandro Tonali started for Spurs in the match. The game continues with further pre-season action.

stories.title: Un énorme panache de fumée noire surplombe un important incendie à l’entreprise Suez recyclerie de Nevers
stories.summary: A massive plume of black smoke looms over a major fire at the Suez recycling plant in Nevers, France, where eight firefighting vehicles and police are deployed. The blaze, believed to have started from a fire of cartons, metals, and papers, has caused no reported injuries among the evacuated employees. The fire has prompted a power outage in the area, with authorities warning of a nearby high-voltage power line. Firefighters are working to contain the fire and restore power to the affected areas.

stories.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly testing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely come with 12GB of RAM, a significant upgrade from the original 8GB. The new model is expected to retain its budget-friendly price tag, despite ongoing component shortages. The updated Neo is expected to be released alongside new iMacs with OLED screens. Apple has not officially confirmed the plans, but industry insiders suggest a refresh is imminent.

stories.title: West Ham sanction Summerville exit as €70m transfer hijack agreed
stories.summary: West Ham have sanctioned Crysencio Summerville’s exit to Saudi Pro League side Al Hilal for €70m after their €70m offer trumped Roma’s bid. The Netherlands international, who scored five Premier League goals last season, will undergo a medical before signing a long-term deal. Summerville joined West Ham from Leeds United in 2024 for over £25m. West Ham have been boosted by Jarrod Bowen agreeing to stay despite interest from Aston Villa and Everton.

stories.title: Mahmood and Streeting accused of making ‘cheap jokes’ about prisoner early release scheme
stories.summary: Justice Secretary Alex Norris was caught on camera being "ribbed" by Defence Secretary Wes Streeting and Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood about the government's early release of prisoners. Streeting and Mahmood have apologized for the comments, saying the issue is serious. The government's early release scheme affects over 12,000 prisoners. Norris will now face scrutiny over the handling of the scheme.

stories.title: Here’s Why Rosalía Is Apologizing to Argentine Fans Following Spain’s World Cup Victory
stories.summary: Rosalía apologized to Argentine fans after Spain’s 2026 FIFA World Cup victory on July 19 in New Jersey. The Spanish singer reposted a controversial Instagram Story referencing her song “La Perla,” which many Latin American followers found offensive. She clarified she didn’t read the caption before sharing it. Her LUX World Tour is set to perform in Buenos Aires on August 1.

stories.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
stories.summary: Liverpool have made an initial approach for Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche, 24, as PSG also pursue the France international. The Reds checked his availability amid ongoing transfer interest. Monaco has rejected multiple PSG offers so far. Liverpool’s next step depends on Monaco’s valuation and Akliouche’s decision.

stories.title: International media in Tuam to tour new forensics facility for Mother and Baby Home excavation
stories.summary: International media toured a new €multi-million forensics facility in Tuam today, linked to excavations at the former Mother and Baby Home. The facility at Toghermore House supports a team of experts analyzing 99 infant remains recovered so far. Authorities briefed reporters on progress as the ongoing dig continues. Further findings are expected as investigations proceed.

stories.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
stories.summary: Prosecutors in Rome sought chat records between Andrea Delmastro and a detained restaurateur linked to mafia charges, but the Senate’s Committee on Authorisation to Proceed rejected the request. Five Star Movement senators occupied government benches for over 30 minutes, demanding release of the logs, while centre-right lawmakers backed the refusal. The centre-left opposed the decision, citing concerns over the request’s vagueness and lack of scope. The committee’s rejection halts further action on the matter.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting these specific nutrients in food policy, rather than the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework. This shift aims to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for healthier food options. The UK government is now expected to review its food policy in response to the report's findings.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and eight people remaining hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 of 163 tested towers have tested positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to investigate and test cooling towers. Further testing is expected to reveal the extent of the outbreak.

stories.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
stories.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna on date after being restrained by police during a confrontation. The autopsy, delayed by hours, will examine possible excessive force and the victim’s pre-existing health conditions. Authorities called officers after Fakir was found in a confused state. Investigators will assess the case amid ongoing scrutiny of police use-of-force protocols.

stories.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
stories.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at age 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at his family’s Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family. Funeral arrangements will remain private, with flowers or memorial trees accepted at the family’s floral shop.
stories.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

stories.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
stories.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire were on course for a win after reaching 109-3, but a dramatic collapse saw them lose five wickets in 14 balls. The turning point came when 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman sent back Georgia Voll for 52. Brave's Sophie Molineux finished with 3-14 to guide her team to victory.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and requested extradition to Russia. The case is part of a larger investigation into a Russian political network, Africa Politology, accused of spreading anti-Western narratives in Angola.

stories.title: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel
stories.summary: Tom Cruise is set to star in a sequel to the 1990 film "Days of Thunder" with Jonathan Levine in talks to direct. The film will be produced by Cruise and Jerry Bruckheimer. Paramount is expected to release the sequel after releasing Levine's next film, "Mr. Irrelevant", on Christmas Day. The sequel's script was written by Will Staples.

stories.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
stories.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory released a new image of the spiral galaxy Messier 94 on June 30, 2026, showcasing its bright inner ring and unique oval-shaped structure. The image combines X-ray data from Chandra with a visible light image from ground-based telescopes. This galaxy features a starburst ring where new stars are forming. The image is now accompanied by a sound experience.

stories.title: Why are cycling's anti-doping tests being carried out at night?
stories.summary: Anti-doping tests at the Tour de France are being conducted at night for the first time, with five teams having riders tested between 11pm and 6am. The unusual testing hours have sparked debate, with some attributing the disrupted sleep of rider Jonas Vingegaard to his crash on the following stage. The move is seen as effective in preventing certain types of doping. The UCI has justified the nocturnal tests as necessary in exceptional circumstances.

stories.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads tributes to Kaylee Hottle
stories.summary: US actress Kaylee Hottle, 18, died in a single-vehicle car crash in Frederick, Maryland on Tuesday. Hottle, who was deaf, appeared in the Godzilla vs Kong film series, alongside Millie Bobby Brown and Rebecca Hall. Tributes poured in from co-stars including Brown, Hall, and Eiza González, remembering her talent and kindness. A sequel to Godzilla vs Kong, Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire, is set to release later this year.

stories.title: Eccles and Salmon given Team Wales honour in Glasgow
stories.summary: Reigning Commonwealth Games champions Rosie Eccles and Daniel Salmon have been chosen as Team Wales' flag and baton bearers for the opening ceremony in Glasgow. Eccles, a 30-year-old boxer from Caldicot, will celebrate her birthday by carrying the flag and leading Wales out at the Hydro on Thursday night. This is her third and final Games, having won silver on the Gold Coast in 2018 and gold in Birmingham. Eccles will now lead a team of athletes onto the stage, a moment she never thought possible.

stories.title: French police formally investigate woman over 2025 killing of Briton
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in France over the 2025 stabbing death of a 65-year-old British-South African mother-of-four, Karen Carter, in the village of Trémolat. Carter was found with stab wounds by a friend near her car in April last year. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She is now facing formal charges following judicial inquiry investigations. The case is ongoing.

stories.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
stories.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
stories.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody" alongside Ben Stiller, marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series. The show follows a disgraced financier awaiting trial, navigating prison politics and confronting consequences. Del Toro and Stiller previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series "Escape at Dannemora." The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

stories.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
stories.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

stories.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
stories.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

stories.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
stories.summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

stories.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
stories.summary: The Trump administration is expected to announce an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

stories.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
stories.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

stories.title: Japan Is Startled by Prime Minister’s Grueling Sleep Schedule
stories.summary: Sanae Takaichi has said she gets just “zero to three hours” of sleep a night, prompting a debate in the country over work-life balance.

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Susumu Tonegawa Dies at 86; Nobel Laureate Showed How Antibodies Fight Infection
stories.summary: The first Japanese scientist to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, he went on to discover how memories are stored, advancing understanding of how the brain works.

offbeat.title: Mapped: Spain braces for 47C heatwave as tourist hotspots warned over record-breaking weather
offbeat.summary: Spain braces for a record-breaking heatwave this week, with temperatures set to hit 47C in the southeastern interior, nearing the country’s all-time high of 47.6C in 2021. The extreme heat, driven by a Saharan "heat dome," will push wildfire risks to "extreme" levels across much of the country. Popular tourist spots like Málaga and Catalonia face highs of 45C and 42C respectively, while parts of Spain could surpass North Africa in heat. Authorities warn the dangerous conditions will persist through the week.

offbeat.title: French modeling scout with links to Epstein found dead
offbeat.summary: A French modeling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein was found dead at his home near Paris on Monday. Daniel Siad, 69, was under investigation in France for alleged rape and human trafficking tied to Epstein. Prosecutors opened a probe into his death and plan an autopsy. Siad had denied the accusations and sought to present his side before passing.

offbeat.title: Will Lionel Messi win the Ballon d'Or, and when will Argentina's World Cup star rejoin Inter Miami?
offbeat.summary: Lionel Messi’s World Cup final loss on July 19, 2026, ended his 2026 Ballon d’Or eligibility, with the award’s evaluation period closing July 31. FIFA’s 21-day rest rule keeps him off Inter Miami’s pitch until at least August 9. The U.S. broadcast of the final drew 63 million viewers, the most for a soccer match in American history. Argentina’s economic openness is also fueling parallel imports, challenging IP rights enforcement.

offbeat.title: Garnacho to Aston Villa: What does Emery see in latest ex-Man Utd flop?
offbeat.summary: Alejandro Garnacho will join Aston Villa on loan from Chelsea with a conditional obligation to make the move permanent. The 22-year-old Argentine winger completed his medical and is finalizing the deal in Birmingham. Villa manager Unai Emery personally requested the signing, with a four-year contract lined up if conditions are met. The move follows Chelsea's record £117 million sale of Morgan Rogers to Villa earlier this summer.

offbeat.title: Jasper Philipsen wins stage 17 of Tour de France, Tadej Pogačar protects lead
offbeat.summary: Belgian Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France on July 22, 2026, from Chambery to Voiron, his first stage victory of the race. Tadej Pogačar retained the overall lead with a 4-minute, 32-second advantage over Remco Evenepoel. Philipsen finished ahead of Mauro Schmid and Olav Kooij in the sprint. Stage 18 on July 23 features a 185.2-kilometer mountain route from Voiron to Orcieres-Merlette.

offbeat.title: Auto e Moto d’Epoca 2026 expands endurance and motorcycle programme in Bologna
offbeat.summary: Auto e Moto d’Epoca 2026 returns to BolognaFiere from October 22-25, expanding its historic vehicle programme with over 5000 cars and bikes across 14 halls. A new Icons of Speed exhibition will feature endurance racing legends like the 1989 Sauber-Mercedes C9 and 2001 Bentley EXP Speed 8. The event expects visitors from 40 countries, highlighting the global collector market.

offbeat.title: Tottenham vs MK Dons LIVE! Pre-season fixture, score, live updates and team news
offbeat.summary: Tottenham beat MK Dons 1-0 in a closed-doors pre-season friendly at Hotspur Way on Wednesday 22 July 2026. Mateus Fernandes scored the only goal with a first-half volley from outside the box. Both clubs used the match to test new signings and squad depth ahead of the new season. Spurs’ Sandro Tonali and Fernandes started together in midfield.

offbeat.title: Four teens to appear in court after ‘serious’ Virginia incident
offbeat.summary: Four juveniles face charges after a violent incident in Virginia, Co Cavan, on July 21, with three people sustaining significant but non-life-threatening injuries. The teens are due in Cavan District Children's Court tomorrow at 10:30am. Gardaí say the probe continues, including searches near Lough Ramor. Authorities have not yet linked this case to separate homicide charges against the same suspects.

offbeat.title: ResilExec Coaching Launches Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™ -- Completing a Full Career Architecture Platform for Mid-Career Leaders in Healthcare and Enterprise IT
offbeat.summary: ResilExec Coaching launched Purpose-Driven Transition™ and Purpose-Driven Continuity™, completing a career advancement platform for mid-career leaders in healthcare and enterprise IT. The programs target Associate Directors through VPs navigating external transitions or post-promotion challenges. Venerable Holdings agreed to transfer $4.5 billion in assets to Venerable’s advisory platform under a deal with Guardian Life Insurance. The consolidation aims to improve fund efficiency and policyholder benefits. Hillsboro, Oregon, unanimously urged the Port of Portland to switch to unleaded fuel at Hillsboro Airport by 2030 to cut toxic emissions. Research warns that rapid renewable energy shifts in developing countries can raise household costs and deepen poverty without proper infrastructure and support.

offbeat.title: Revealed: What Otamendi told Rodri in argument after World Cup final
offbeat.summary: Nicolás Otamendi and Rodri argued heatedly after the 2026 World Cup final, with Otamendi criticizing Rodri for complaining to officials. Rodri, named Golden Ball winner, responded by telling Otamendi to show respect before they were separated. Real Madrid have reignited interest in Rodri after his tournament-winning performances. Manchester City continue contract talks with the Spain midfielder as his future remains uncertain.

offbeat.title: Testspiele am Mittwoch: MSV, Hilden und die halbe Oberliga spielen
offbeat.summary: MSV Duisburg plays against Beerschot VA from Belgium at home in Duisburg on Wednesday, July 22, at 18:00. The match is a test game for the Drittligist ahead of the new Drittliga season. The game will be broadcast live on the YouTube channel of the 3. Liga.

offbeat.title: "Un cattivo con cui la Juve ha sbagliato...": così l'Italia ha fatto di Romero un campione
offbeat.summary: Cristian Romero, the Argentine defender who nearly joined Juventus in 2018 for €20 million, is now a top target for Inter Milan to strengthen their backline. The 25-year-old, who played six matches at the 2022 World Cup, was sold by Atalanta to Tottenham for €54 million in 2021 after initially joining for around €20 million. Inter aims to secure Romero as part of their defensive upgrades.

offbeat.title: Amistoso en Paterna, cuando Gourlay se topó con la realidad
offbeat.summary: Valencia CF faces Eldense in its second preseason friendly at Paterna’s Antonio Puchades stadium at 8:00 p.m. on Wednesday, July 22. The match, broadcast live on À Punt, follows a closed-door opener against Petro Luanda. Corberán’s side, including new signings, aims to test cohesion before the third game against Castellón on Saturday.

offbeat.title: Play More of the Games You Love, Wherever You Play with XBOX Backward Compatibility on PC
offbeat.summary: Microsoft is expanding its Xbox backward compatibility efforts by bringing four classic Xbox games to PC, including Blinx: The Time Sweeper, Conker: Live and Reloaded, Crimson Skies: High Road to Revenge, and Fuzion Frenzy. The games are available for purchase and download on PC, and also included with all Xbox Game Pass plans. Players can now play these games on PC and supported handheld devices, with added features such as customizable graphics settings and achievements coming in the coming months. This marks the beginning of a broader effort to preserve Xbox games from the past and bring them to PC over time.

offbeat.title: Gearóid Hegarty enjoying special days alongside his best friends
offbeat.summary: Limerick’s Gearóid Hegarty and teammates celebrated their sixth All-Ireland senior hurling title on July 22, 2026, marking a historic sixth medal for the core group. Hegarty’s assist to David Reidy in the final highlighted the tight-knit bond within the squad, with Reidy and Seán Finn set to be groomsmen at Hegarty’s wedding in Portugal soon. The team’s remarkable durability has strengthened their friendship, with many players part of the journey since 2017. They reunite mid-winter after a 14-game season.

offbeat.title: Beliebte ARD-Quizsendung: Leunaer bei „Gefragt - Gejagt“ - Nach Schicksalsschlag in Jubiläumsshow
offbeat.summary: A 61-year-old man from Leuna has made it to the final of the popular ARD quiz show "Gefragt - Gejagt" after being given a second chance at life. The show celebrated its 1000th episode this week, marking 14 years of its unique concept. The contestant, who has been giving lectures at the Planetarium Merseburg, will now compete in the final. The outcome of the competition will be revealed in the next episode.

offbeat.title: Confinan Villa del Prado por un incendio forestal originado en Almorox (Toledo)
offbeat.summary: A wildfire broke out Wednesday afternoon in Almorox, Toledo, forcing residents of Villa del Prado in Madrid to shelter in place. Authorities evacuated two nearby housing developments and cut the N-403 between Cadalso de los Vidrios and Almorox. Fire crews from Madrid and Castilla-La Mancha are battling the blaze with 19 ground and air units. Residents are advised to avoid the M-501 and M-507 roads as operations continue.

offbeat.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
offbeat.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall of their booked holiday home in Co Wexford last weekend. Therese, who reserved the entire property via Booking.com for an overnight stay with her partner and two children, said the owner assured her he wouldn’t be present upon their return. Booking.com confirmed the property has been suspended pending an ongoing investigation. Authorities have not yet commented on the incident.

offbeat.title: USA: New Jersey registrierte versehentlich mehr als 6500 Nicht-US-Bürger als Wähler
offbeat.summary: New Jersey officials reported a software error that mistakenly registered over 6,600 non-U.S. citizens as voters ahead of the 2024 presidential election. Governor Mikie Sherrill called the mistake unacceptable and said fewer than 400 of those registered actually voted. The error occurred between June 2023 and June 2024 during driver’s license and ID applications. State officials are reviewing safeguards ahead of upcoming elections.

offbeat.title: Manuel Barragán vuelve a actuar después de casi perder la vida: "No hay nada que hacer"
offbeat.summary: Manuel Barragán, conocido por su personaje cómico, superó un grave accidente tras 17 días intubado en UCI por un paro cardíaco y choque contra una farola. El cómico pasó 40 días hospitalizado tras sufrir un aneurisma en la aorta que agravó su infarto. Su familia vivió angustia extrema durante su recuperación, que él califica de milagro. Barragán ya reanudó su actividad y agradeció al equipo médico que lo atendió.

offbeat.title: Caída de Instagram: la app de Meta registra problemas de funcionamiento en España
offbeat.summary: Instagram experienced widespread disruptions in Spain on July 22, with users reporting login failures, missing posts, and message sending issues starting around 4:00 PM local time. Downdetector logged over 200 complaints, primarily affecting the mobile app. Service reportedly stabilized by 5:45 PM, though Meta has not commented on the cause. Users are advised to check Downdetector for real-time updates if issues persist.

offbeat.title: Frankreich: Waldbrand rast auf Cotignac und andere Orte zu
offbeat.summary: A wildfire in southern France’s Var department forced nearly 400 people to evacuate as flames raced toward Cotignac and two other towns, scorching 1,700 hectares in hours. Authorities deployed nearly 1,000 firefighters and eight aircraft to battle the blaze, which moved unpredictably down Gros Bessillon mountain. Separately, two firefighters died in a Bordeaux-area blaze that trapped them in their vehicle amid extreme fire conditions. Investigations are underway into both fires’ origins.

offbeat.title: En direct Football. Le quatrième but du FC Metz face au FC Sochaux-Montbéliard : suivez le sixième match de préparation des Grenats en direct
offbeat.summary: FC Metz beat FC Sochaux-Montbéliard 4-1 in a pre-season friendly on July 22, 2026, at Biesheim Municipal Stadium. Pape Moussa Fall scored a hat-trick and Jessy Deminguet added the fourth goal for Metz. Sochaux’s Mathis Clairicia netted the consolation strike in the 78th minute. Metz’s strong performance follows two opening wins in their summer preparation.

offbeat.title: Tottenham vs. MK Dons: how to watch preseason friendly
offbeat.summary: Tottenham Hotspur hosted MK Dons in a closed-door preseason friendly today at Hotspur Way training center. Portuguese midfielder Mateus Fernandes scored the opening goal with a stunning volley from outside the box. The match, part of preseason preparations, was only available to watch via SpursPLAY. Both Fernandes and Sandro Tonali started for Spurs under manager Roberto De Zerbi.

offbeat.title: Condannata Ilaria Salis: collaboratori licenziati senza giusta causa, risarcimento da 300mila euro
offbeat.summary: Italian Eurodeputy Ilaria Salis has been condemned to pay 300,000 euros in damages to two former collaborators she fired without cause. The Milan court's March ruling, in absentia, accepted the ex-employees' claims, citing unjustified terminations after only a few months of work. Salis, elected in June 2024, will present an appeal.

offbeat.title: Garnacho, Rogers and two clubs trying to balance the books
offbeat.summary: Chelsea is close to breaching Uefa's financial rules, having spent between £164m and £210m on player transfers since Xabi Alonso became manager. The club has agreed to a deal with Aston Villa for the loan of Alejandro Garnacho, with a conditional obligation to buy the 22-year-old for a reported £42.7m. This deal comes as Chelsea's financial position has improved, with the club generating a significant amount of revenue from player sales. The club's transfer activity will be closely monitored by Uefa as they continue to balance their books.

offbeat.title: Hospital apologises after man's death in Galway
offbeat.summary: Henrique Coelho, 26, died on 20 January 2024, two days after being discharged from Galway University Hospital’s emergency department despite complaints of shortness of breath and chest pain. The High Court awarded his family €35,000 in damages for negligence after a postmortem revealed critical omissions in his care. The hospital issued a formal apology to the family in May 2026. Authorities have not indicated further disciplinary or systemic reviews.

offbeat.title: Xandro Meurisse percuté par un automobiliste après le contre-la-montre du Tour de France : « La voiture l'a projeté »
offbeat.summary: Xandro Meurisse, 34 ans, a été percuté par une voiture mardi après le contre-la-montre entre Évian-les-Bains et Thonon-les-Bains. Le Belge de l’équipe Pinarello-Q36.5, qui n’a pas subi de fracture mais souffre de brûlures, a pris le départ de la 17e étape ce mercredi. Son manager a confirmé l’incident, décrivant un choc violent projetant le coureur. Aucune enquête n’a encore été ouverte sur les circonstances de l’accident.

offbeat.title: Bivacchi in montagna usati come alberghi: il Cai lancia l’allarme sugli abusi sulle Dolomiti
offbeat.summary: Il Club Alpino Italiano lancia l’allarme per l’abuso crescente dei bivacchi sulle Dolomiti, usati sempre più come alloggi improvvisati invece che come rifugi di emergenza. Episodi di vandalismo, rifiuti e occupazioni improprie hanno costretto a interventi di manutenzione straordinari, con costi in aumento. Il 24 luglio a Pieve di Cadore si terrà un convegno nazionale per discutere soluzioni, tra cui la revisione delle regole d’uso. Le strutture restano aperte senza prenotazione, accessibili solo in caso di necessità reale.

offbeat.title: Le PSG creuse l’option Charles De Ketelaere
offbeat.summary: Paris Saint-Germain is accelerating transfer plans with the 2026 World Cup over, targeting midfielder Charles De Ketelaere. The club aims to strengthen its squad before key fixtures on August 12 against Aston Villa in the UEFA Super Cup and August 16 against Lens in the Trophée des Champions. De Ketelaere remains a priority as PSG seeks reinforcements to integrate into Luis Enrique’s system. Monaco’s asking price has so far blocked potential moves.

offbeat.title: Mamdani U-turn on threat to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
offbeat.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani reversed his pledge to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, admitting he lacks authority to enforce the International Criminal Court’s 2024 arrest warrant. Mamdani called on US federal authorities to act, as the US is not an ICC member, while Netanyahu plans a September UN visit. The mayor reiterated his stance that Netanyahu is unwelcome in the city.

offbeat.title: Mannoia a De Gregori: "Su Gaza non avere idee chiare non te lo perdono"
offbeat.summary: Fiorella Mannoia ha criticato Francesco De Gregori per le sue dichiarazioni su Gaza, definendole imperdonabili di fronte al conflitto in corso. La cantante ha espresso il suo disappunto durante il festival Gaberiana a Firenze, sottolineando la necessità di chiarezza su temi di portata storica. Mannoia, amica e collega di De Gregori, ha ricordato il suo impegno politico nelle canzoni. Il dibattito riflette le tensioni su come gli artisti dovrebbero affrontare questioni sociali urgenti.

offbeat.title: 'Would Beckham do it?' - Fury stands by Thailand fight
offbeat.summary: Tyson Fury will face Mariusz Wach in a non-televised fight in Pattaya, Thailand on Friday, with a win setting up a potential showdown with Anthony Joshua. Fury, 37, has won 35 pro fights, with two defeats and one draw. The fight will not be broadcast live, but a win will put Fury one step closer to facing Joshua in November. Fury's comments suggest he is focused on his upcoming bout, rather than seeking a more lucrative event elsewhere.

offbeat.title: Un secolo di passione azzurra: la sfilata dei campioni e lo show del Plebiscito per celebrare i 100 anni del Napoli
offbeat.summary: Napoli’s SSC club marks its 100th anniversary with a free citywide festival on August 1. The celebration starts at 7:26 p.m. from Piazza Carità, the site of the club’s first headquarters, with church bells and blue smoke. A one-kilometer parade will wind through central streets, led by club president Aurelio De Laurentiis and local leaders. The event blends sport, music, and tradition to honor the club’s deep ties to the city and its global fanbase.

offbeat.title: Wilfred Genee-beelden nep? ‘Nee, dan zou hij gelijk ontkennen!’
offbeat.summary: Wilfred Genee faces fresh scrutiny after viral kroegbeelden showed him with a younger woman in Soest. Johan Derksen defended him in Groeten uit Grolloo, calling the encounter a harmless hug with his ex-children’s nanny. Genee has not responded to the footage aired by RoddelPraat. Mainstream pundits now weigh in as the debate intensifies.

offbeat.title: Mercato : accord en vue entre l'AS Monaco et le FC Nantes pour Matthis Abline
offbeat.summary: Matthis Abline, a 23-year-old attacker, is close to joining AS Monaco in a record transfer deal worth around 30 million euros, surpassing the previous record held by Nathan Zézé. Abline has already agreed to sign with Monaco, and the two clubs are nearing an agreement after months of negotiations. This deal would be a significant transaction in the history of FC Nantes, where Abline currently plays. The transfer is expected to be finalized soon.

offbeat.title: Preview: Levski Sofia vs Universitatea Craiova - prediction, team news, lineups
offbeat.summary: Levski Sofia hosts Universitatea Craiova at Georgi Asparuhov Stadium for the first leg of their Champions League second qualifying round tie on Wednesday. The Bulgarian champions, who reached this stage with a 5-1 aggregate victory over Borac Banja Luka, will look to make home advantage count. Levski Sofia dominated their previous qualifier, winning 4-0 in the second leg. A win for Levski Sofia would set them up well for the return leg in Romania.

offbeat.title: Will Britain’s summer heatwave outdo 1976? Here’s what the data says
offbeat.summary: Britain’s current heatwave has seen 31 days over 25C so far this summer, nearing the 33 recorded in the record-breaking 1976 heatwave. The dry spell has left eastern and north-eastern England with just half their usual rainfall, while hosepipe bans now cover over two million people. Forecasters warn high pressure will keep conditions hot and largely rain-free for at least another week, with temperatures potentially hitting 37C by July 31. The Met Office says very warm weather could persist into early August, raising the risk of further heat warnings.

offbeat.title: Gemeinsam gesellschaftliche Resilienz stärken
offbeat.summary: Up to eight German cities and municipalities will join a new resilience project starting October 2026. The initiative aims to strengthen crisis preparedness by involving local organizations and citizens in a six-month participatory process. Separately, a June workshop in Neustadt Holstein trained 14 people with Multiple Sclerosis in resilience techniques to better manage daily challenges. Both efforts focus on building inner strength to handle stress and adversity.

offbeat.title: INFO OUEST-FRANCE. À Rennes, une femme de 80 ans violée dans son hall d’immeuble par un jeune de 16 ans
offbeat.summary: A 16-year-old was arrested in Rennes on July 21, 2026, for the July 4 rape of an 80-year-old woman in her apartment building’s lobby. Police found cocaine in his possession during the arrest. The suspect, a foreign minor, remains in custody with a court hearing set for July 23. Authorities identified him through DNA evidence.

offbeat.title: L'estate bollente di Bastoni: il mercato, le parole dell'agente, l'Inter che se lo tiene stretto
offbeat.summary: Alessandro Bastoni remains Inter Milan’s key defender as Saudi club Al-Hilal explores a potential move. No formal offer has been made, and Bastoni is currently considered untouchable by the Nerazzurri. The Saudi transfer rules require Al-Hilal to sell a foreign player before signing another, delaying any immediate deal. Inter’s focus remains on reinforcing their backline, though a record bid could force a rethink.

offbeat.title: Los cazas F-16 ucranianos pasan a la ofensiva: EEUU confirma el derribo de un Su-35 ruso
offbeat.summary: Un F-16 ucraniano derribó un caza ruso Su-35S en combate aéreo el 8 de julio, confirmaron fuentes estadounidenses. El general Dan Caine, jefe del Estado Mayor Conjunto de EEUU, validó el hito durante una audiencia en el Senado el 21 de julio. Este primer derribo aire-aire marca un giro en la guerra, donde Ucrania emplea cazas occidentales para misiones ofensivas. La industria ucraniana avanza en capacidades de defensa aérea y combate aéreo.

offbeat.title: Kyle Kelly and Omari Patrick: Forest Green Rovers sign duo
offbeat.summary: Forest Green Rovers have signed midfielder Kyle Kelly on a six-month loan from Liverpool and forward Omari Patrick on a permanent deal. Kelly, 20, has eight caps for St Kitts and Nevis, while Patrick, 29, scored seven goals for Tranmere last season. Both moves were confirmed ahead of the new campaign. Kelly will join the squad immediately as they prepare for their opening fixtures.

offbeat.title: AZ oefent al 23 jaar in Dirkshorn: "Gras moet precies 2,1 centimeter lang zijn"
offbeat.summary: AZ has trained in Dirkshorn for 23 years, preparing for tonight’s friendly against KRC Genk on a pitch with grass precisely 2.1 centimeters tall. The match starts at 18:30 as AZ aims to sharpen form ahead of the Johan Cruijff Schaal on August 2. After mixed results against Anderlecht and Zulte Waregem, the Alkmaarders seek consistency before facing PSV in the season opener.

offbeat.title: The PSOE backs Zapatero and leaves his future in the hands of Justice: "The truth will come to light"
offbeat.summary: Spain’s Socialist Party (PSOE) reaffirmed its support for former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero on Wednesday amid accusations tied to the Plus Ultra airline rescue, urging the judiciary to determine the facts. PSOE spokesperson Montse Mínguez stated the party has no reason to doubt Zapatero’s account and emphasized the ongoing judicial investigation will clarify the allegations. Agriculture Minister Luis Planas linked recent claims by businessman Julio Martínez to a defense strategy in the case, reiterating Zapatero’s presumption of innocence. The Plus Ultra rescue unfolded during the pandemic under EU-backed liquidity measures.

offbeat.title: Vijftien gewonden bij botsing trams op Erasmusbrug in Rotterdam, 'vreselijk ongeluk'
offbeat.summary: Two trams collided on Rotterdam’s Erasmus Bridge around 11:10 AM Wednesday, leaving 15 injured, including one in critical condition. Emergency crews freed a trapped passenger and treated others on-site or at area hospitals. The crash damaged both trams and closed the bridge to all traffic. Authorities confirmed the toll was revised downward from earlier reports.

offbeat.title: Watch: Mateus Fernandes scores stunning goal on Tottenham Hotspur debut vs MK Dons
offbeat.summary: Tottenham Hotspur midfielder Mateus Fernandes scored a stunning goal on his debut during a pre-season friendly against MK Dons on July 27. The Portuguese player struck from outside the box with a volley to open the scoring. Both Fernandes and Sandro Tonali started for Spurs in the match. The game continues with further pre-season action.

offbeat.title: Un énorme panache de fumée noire surplombe un important incendie à l’entreprise Suez recyclerie de Nevers
offbeat.summary: A massive plume of black smoke looms over a major fire at the Suez recycling plant in Nevers, France, where eight firefighting vehicles and police are deployed. The blaze, believed to have started from a fire of cartons, metals, and papers, has caused no reported injuries among the evacuated employees. The fire has prompted a power outage in the area, with authorities warning of a nearby high-voltage power line. Firefighters are working to contain the fire and restore power to the affected areas.

offbeat.title: West Ham sanction Summerville exit as €70m transfer hijack agreed
offbeat.summary: West Ham have sanctioned Crysencio Summerville’s exit to Saudi Pro League side Al Hilal for €70m after their €70m offer trumped Roma’s bid. The Netherlands international, who scored five Premier League goals last season, will undergo a medical before signing a long-term deal. Summerville joined West Ham from Leeds United in 2024 for over £25m. West Ham have been boosted by Jarrod Bowen agreeing to stay despite interest from Aston Villa and Everton.

offbeat.title: Here’s Why Rosalía Is Apologizing to Argentine Fans Following Spain’s World Cup Victory
offbeat.summary: Rosalía apologized to Argentine fans after Spain’s 2026 FIFA World Cup victory on July 19 in New Jersey. The Spanish singer reposted a controversial Instagram Story referencing her song “La Perla,” which many Latin American followers found offensive. She clarified she didn’t read the caption before sharing it. Her LUX World Tour is set to perform in Buenos Aires on August 1.

offbeat.title: Maghnes Akliouche transfer news: Liverpool make initial approach for Monaco winger amid PSG interest
offbeat.summary: Liverpool have made an initial approach for Monaco winger Maghnes Akliouche, 24, as PSG also pursue the France international. The Reds checked his availability amid ongoing transfer interest. Monaco has rejected multiple PSG offers so far. Liverpool’s next step depends on Monaco’s valuation and Akliouche’s decision.

offbeat.title: International media in Tuam to tour new forensics facility for Mother and Baby Home excavation
offbeat.summary: International media toured a new €multi-million forensics facility in Tuam today, linked to excavations at the former Mother and Baby Home. The facility at Toghermore House supports a team of experts analyzing 99 infant remains recovered so far. Authorities briefed reporters on progress as the ongoing dig continues. Further findings are expected as investigations proceed.

offbeat.title: Delmastro: the Executive Committee has rejected the prosecutors’ request to obtain the chat records. The M5S occupies the government benches
offbeat.summary: Prosecutors in Rome sought chat records between Andrea Delmastro and a detained restaurateur linked to mafia charges, but the Senate’s Committee on Authorisation to Proceed rejected the request. Five Star Movement senators occupied government benches for over 30 minutes, demanding release of the logs, while centre-right lawmakers backed the refusal. The centre-left opposed the decision, citing concerns over the request’s vagueness and lack of scope. The committee’s rejection halts further action on the matter.

offbeat.title: Morte di Abderrahim Fakir a Bologna: l’autopsia e il nodo dello scudo penale
offbeat.summary: A 43-year-old Moroccan man, Abderrahim Fakir, died in Bologna on date after being restrained by police during a confrontation. The autopsy, delayed by hours, will examine possible excessive force and the victim’s pre-existing health conditions. Authorities called officers after Fakir was found in a confused state. Investigators will assess the case amid ongoing scrutiny of police use-of-force protocols.

offbeat.title: Obituary for David J. Ascione
offbeat.summary: David J. Ascione, known as D.J., died on July 19, 2026, at age 62 in Toms River, New Jersey. A lifelong resident, he worked at his family’s Ascione Florist and Greenhouse on Route 9 and served with the Pleasant Plains Volunteer Fire Company. He is survived by his mother, sister, aunt, uncle, and extended family. Funeral arrangements will remain private, with flowers or memorial trees accepted at the family’s floral shop.
offbeat.sourceName: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals
sources: Timothy E. Ryan Home For Funerals

offbeat.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
offbeat.summary: Two Russians, Igor Ratchin and Lev Lakshtanov, have been sentenced to 11 and 8 years in prison respectively for terrorism and spying in Angola's capital, Luanda. They were found guilty of stirring up anti-government protests, conducting disinformation campaigns and attempting to interfere in next year's presidential election. The court's decision comes after the defendants denied the charges and requested extradition to Russia. The case is part of a larger investigation into a Russian political network, Africa Politology, accused of spreading anti-Western narratives in Angola.

offbeat.title: French police formally investigate woman over 2025 killing of Briton
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in France over the 2025 stabbing death of a 65-year-old British-South African mother-of-four, Karen Carter, in the village of Trémolat. Carter was found with stab wounds by a friend near her car in April last year. The woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She is now facing formal charges following judicial inquiry investigations. The case is ongoing.

tech.title: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with more RAM
tech.summary: Apple is reportedly testing an updated MacBook Neo with a new A19 Pro processor, which will likely come with 12GB of RAM, a significant upgrade from the original 8GB. The new model is expected to retain its budget-friendly price tag, despite ongoing component shortages. The updated Neo is expected to be released alongside new iMacs with OLED screens. Apple has not officially confirmed the plans, but industry insiders suggest a refresh is imminent.

tech.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
tech.summary: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.

tech.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Hacked Into Hugging Face, a Digital Library
tech.summary: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.

politics.title: Mahmood and Streeting accused of making ‘cheap jokes’ about prisoner early release scheme
politics.summary: Justice Secretary Alex Norris was caught on camera being "ribbed" by Defence Secretary Wes Streeting and Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood about the government's early release of prisoners. Streeting and Mahmood have apologized for the comments, saying the issue is serious. The government's early release scheme affects over 12,000 prisoners. Norris will now face scrutiny over the handling of the scheme.

politics.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
politics.summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

politics.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
politics.summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report has found that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting these specific nutrients in food policy, rather than the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework. This shift aims to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for healthier food options. The UK government is now expected to review its food policy in response to the report's findings.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City due to legionnaires' disease, with 82 cases identified and eight people remaining hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to cooling towers on Manhattan's Upper East Side, where 34 of 163 tested towers have tested positive for live legionella bacteria. The city's health department is continuing to investigate and test cooling towers. Further testing is expected to reveal the extent of the outbreak.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

sports.title: Brave fightback against Fire to make winning start
sports.summary: Southern Brave secured an eight-run win over Welsh Fire in the Hundred women's competition, reaching 126-7 in 100 balls. Welsh Fire were on course for a win after reaching 109-3, but a dramatic collapse saw them lose five wickets in 14 balls. The turning point came when 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman sent back Georgia Voll for 52. Brave's Sophie Molineux finished with 3-14 to guide her team to victory.

sports.title: Why are cycling's anti-doping tests being carried out at night?
sports.summary: Anti-doping tests at the Tour de France are being conducted at night for the first time, with five teams having riders tested between 11pm and 6am. The unusual testing hours have sparked debate, with some attributing the disrupted sleep of rider Jonas Vingegaard to his crash on the following stage. The move is seen as effective in preventing certain types of doping. The UCI has justified the nocturnal tests as necessary in exceptional circumstances.

sports.title: Eccles and Salmon given Team Wales honour in Glasgow
sports.summary: Reigning Commonwealth Games champions Rosie Eccles and Daniel Salmon have been chosen as Team Wales' flag and baton bearers for the opening ceremony in Glasgow. Eccles, a 30-year-old boxer from Caldicot, will celebrate her birthday by carrying the flag and leading Wales out at the Hydro on Thursday night. This is her third and final Games, having won silver on the Gold Coast in 2018 and gold in Birmingham. Eccles will now lead a team of athletes onto the stage, a moment she never thought possible.

entertainment.title: ‘Days of Thunder 2’: Jonathan Levine in Talks to Direct Tom Cruise in Paramount Sequel
entertainment.summary: Tom Cruise is set to star in a sequel to the 1990 film "Days of Thunder" with Jonathan Levine in talks to direct. The film will be produced by Cruise and Jerry Bruckheimer. Paramount is expected to release the sequel after releasing Levine's next film, "Mr. Irrelevant", on Christmas Day. The sequel's script was written by Will Staples.

entertainment.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads tributes to Kaylee Hottle
entertainment.summary: US actress Kaylee Hottle, 18, died in a single-vehicle car crash in Frederick, Maryland on Tuesday. Hottle, who was deaf, appeared in the Godzilla vs Kong film series, alongside Millie Bobby Brown and Rebecca Hall. Tributes poured in from co-stars including Brown, Hall, and Eiza González, remembering her talent and kindness. A sequel to Godzilla vs Kong, Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire, is set to release later this year.

entertainment.title: Benicio Del Toro Joins Ben Stiller in Apple TV Comedy ‘Protective Custody’
entertainment.summary: Benicio Del Toro is set to star in the Apple TV comedy series "Protective Custody" alongside Ben Stiller, marking Del Toro's first lead role in a comedy series. The show follows a disgraced financier awaiting trial, navigating prison politics and confronting consequences. Del Toro and Stiller previously collaborated on the Showtime limited series "Escape at Dannemora." The series is expected to premiere on Apple TV.

facts.title: A New Look – and Sound – for Messier 94
facts.summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory released a new image of the spiral galaxy Messier 94 on June 30, 2026, showcasing its bright inner ring and unique oval-shaped structure. The image combines X-ray data from Chandra with a visible light image from ground-based telescopes. This galaxy features a starburst ring where new stars are forming. The image is now accompanied by a sound experience.

facts.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
facts.summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

facts.title: Susumu Tonegawa Dies at 86; Nobel Laureate Showed How Antibodies Fight Infection
facts.summary: The first Japanese scientist to win the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, he went on to discover how memories are stored, advancing understanding of how the brain works.

breaking.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
breaking.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

breaking.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
breaking.summary: The Trump administration is expected to announce an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

breaking.title: Japan Is Startled by Prime Minister’s Grueling Sleep Schedule
breaking.summary: Sanae Takaichi has said she gets just “zero to three hours” of sleep a night, prompting a debate in the country over work-life balance.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

crisis.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
crisis.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.
